claims.claim: We investigate whether lightweight, context-derived dynamic grammar constraints applied at decoding time can recover extraction accuracy in a retrieval-augmented evaluation setting where a small language model (Phi-4-mini) produces correct internal representations but emits incorrectly formatted or hallucinated outputs.

evidence_refs.quote: This report documents a progressive series of experiments on Phi-4-mini that test whether the extraction accuracy gap can be closed not by retraining or modifying the retrieval pipeline, but by applying lightweight, context-derived grammar constraints at decoding time.
